intro: The most concrete split between Adapty and Orb is the pricing model: Adapty runs on subscription from $0/mo, with a free tier, while Orb uses usage-based pricing from $1. That shapes how predictable your monthly spend is and how much of the cost sits in infrastructure versus per-seat or per-event fees. Beyond the headline, Adapty carries medium lock-in (subscription sdk wraps app store/play; purchase data exportable) and subscription from $0/mo, with a free tier; Orb carries medium lock-in (billing data exportable) and usage-based pricing from $1. Adapty is a fit when you are saas. Orb is a fit when you are saas. The honest trade-off: Adapty is explicitly not for simple projects; Orb is explicitly not for simple projects. On the product side, Adapty is described as In-app subscription SDK for iOS and Android with paywall A/B testing, revenue analytics, and purchase validation, and Orb is described as Developer-first subscription and usage billing platform with a flexible pricing model API and Stripe-compatible webhooks.
quick_take: Adapty is for saas; Orb is for saas; decide on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose Adapty if you need a free tier to start before committing budget, and your use case matches saas.
verdict_b: Choose Orb if its usage pricing model fits your budget shape better than Adapty's subscription model, and you are saas.

faq.a: Adapty starts at $0/mo; Orb starts at $1. On entry price alone that is the comparison, but real cost scales with users, events or volume, which the entry prices do not capture. Check each vendor's full tier list before committing.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Adapty to Orb?
faq.a: Migration from Adapty to Orb is feasible but not trivial: Adapty has medium lock-in (subscription sdk wraps app store/play; purchase data exportable). Core data can move; integrations and workflow logic need rebuild. Allow for a parallel-run period.

On our scoring Orb rates 5/5 for developer experience versus 4/5 for Adapty. That suggests Orb feels more polished for everyday developer tasks. We still recommend a short hands-on trial on your stack before standardising.

faq.q: Is Orb a good alternative to Adapty?
faq.a: Orb targets the same best-fit audience as Adapty (saas). Note that Orb is explicitly not for simple projects, so it only replaces Adapty if you are outside that exclusion. Pricing models differ (subscription vs usage), which may shift total cost.
